response_text:
To find out how many small bags John can make, we can follow these steps:

1.  **Calculate the total weight of the M&Ms:**
    John has 3 large bags, and each weighs 10 ounces.
    $3 \text{ bags} \times 10 \text{ ounces/bag} = 30 \text{ ounces}$

2.  **Calculate the total number of M&Ms:**
    There are 30 M&Ms in each ounce.
    $30 \text{ ounces} \times 30 \text{ M&Ms/ounce} = 900 \text{ M&Ms}$

3.  **Calculate the number of small bags:**
    John puts 10 M&Ms in each small bag.
    $900 \text{ M&Ms} \div 10 \text{ M&Ms/bag} = 90 \text{ bags}$

**John can make 90 small bags.**
